replication_study.hypothesis: H4-Replication: In an independent military sample, higher Openness to Experience will be positively associated with greater Leadership-Shaping Experiences (standardised β > 0).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Popper Replication Data (2022) – Correlations & CFA/SEM files
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single cross-sectional collection; independent from original 2009 sample.
data_plan.access_details: CSV files provided in replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/

qualification.explanation: The dataset contains the same psychological scales (attachment, trait anxiety, openness to experience, leadership-shaping experiences) scored and parcelled exactly as in the original article, allowing estimation of the identical structural path of interest while providing a new independent sample.
qualification.similarity_to_original: • Variables Oppenness_Total, Open_Par1-3 and Lead_Par1-3 replicate the original constructs (post_registration.json ‘method’ section; replication CSV column names).  • Parcelling structure (3 parcels per latent) matches original AMOS specification (post_registration.json ‘steps’ #4; R script parcel names).
qualification.deviation_from_original: • Sample size 215 vs original 286; labelled 2022 replication not the 2005-2006 IDF cohort (replication CSV meta: 215 rows; original data section sample_size=286).  • Additional demographics (Age, Education, Military_Tenure) not analysed in original study.

data_plan.notes: The correlations file (Popper Data for Correlations.csv) has two trailing empty columns and retains total-scale scores, while Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv contains parcels for SEM. Missing values are coded as NA. No codebook supplied; variable names are self-explanatory.

planned_method.steps:
1) Import correlation dataset and CFA/SEM dataset from /app/data.
2) Inspect and clean (<5% missing) – listwise delete for focal variables.
3) Compute descriptive statistics and Pearson correlation between Oppenness_Total and Leader_Exp_Total.
4) Fit SEM Model1 from original paper using lavaan on parcel dataset, extracting path coefficient OPEN → LEAD.
5) Compare fit indices to original; record standardised β and p-value.
6) Sensitivity checks: linear regression LEAD_Total ~ OPEN_Total with controls Age, Education, Military_Tenure.
planned_method.models: a) Structural Equation Model (4-factor mediation) identical to original specification; b) OLS regression robustness test.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Leadership-Shaping Experiences (latent LEAD, or Leader_Exp_Total for robustness).
planned_method.independent_variables: Openness to Experience (latent OPEN, or Oppenness_Total).
planned_method.control_variables: None in primary SEM; Age, Education, Military_Tenure in robustness regression.
planned_method.tools_software: R (>=4.2) with lavaan, psych, semPlot, apaTables, lavaanPlot, tidyverse.

planned_estimation_and_test.test: z-test provided by lavaan; significance at α=0.05 two-tailed.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion on variables in each model.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: No adjustment — single focal path; alternative models treated as robustness.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replicated if β is positive and p < .05; magnitude within ±0.10 of original 0.34 considered close replication.

files.Popper_Replication.R: New driver script that (1) reads both CSV files from /app/data, (2) cleans data, (3) reproduces correlation table, (4) fits SEM Model1, (5) outputs coefficient table and fit measures to /app/data/results.txt.
files.Popper Replication R Script File.R: Original replication code provided; referenced for model syntax but paths need adaptation to read CSV and save within /app/data.

codebase.notes: Original script defines data frames that presuppose interactive loading; the new driver adds explicit read.csv(...) statements and ensures all output paths comply with /app/data. Requires lavaan, psych, semPlot, apaTables, lavaanPlot.

analysis.instructions: In container: Rscript /app/code/Popper_Replication.R. The script loads datasets, performs SEM, saves results table and model fit in /app/data.
analysis.comparison_metrics: 1) Standardised β (OPEN→LEAD) compared to original 0.34. 2) p-value significance. 3) Model fit indices (CFI, RMSEA) relative to original benchmarks.
